Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description: Checks for suspect pages indicating corruption
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3))
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Run DBCC CHECKDB immediately and restore from backups if needed

Name: CPU w/Odd Number of Cores
Description: The server has an odd number of CPU cores available to SQL Server. This can lead to inefficiencies in parallel query execution since SQL Server parallelism works best with an even number of cores for balanced thread distribution.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        
SELECT 198 AS CheckID,
                  NULL AS DatabaseName,
                  10 AS Priority,
                  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
                  'CPU w/Odd Number of Cores' AS Finding,
                  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/oddity' AS URL,
                  'Node ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), parent_node_id) + ' has ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), COUNT(cpu_id))
                  + CASE WHEN COUNT(cpu_id) = 1 THEN ' core assigned to it. This is a really bad NUMA configuration.'
                         ELSE ' cores assigned to it. This is a really bad NUMA configuration.'
                    END AS Details
           FROM   sys.dm_os_schedulers
           WHERE  is_online = 1
                  AND scheduler_id < 255
                  AND parent_node_id < 64
      AND EXISTS (
         SELECT 1
         FROM ( SELECT    memory_node_id, SUM(online_scheduler_count) AS schedulers
                FROM      sys.dm_os_nodes
                WHERE     memory_node_id < 64
                GROUP  BY memory_node_id ) AS nodes
             HAVING MIN(nodes.schedulers) <> MAX(nodes.schedulers)
         )
           GROUP BY parent_node_id,
                  is_online
           HAVING ( COUNT(cpu_id) + 2 ) % 2 = 1


  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Performance. More info: https://www.brentozar.com/go/oddity

Name: Wait Stats Cleared Recently
Description: SQL Server wait statistics have been cleared recently (via DBCC SQLPERF or a server restart). Cleared wait stats provide an incomplete picture of historical wait activity, hindering performance analysis and trending.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
     SELECT 205 AS CheckID ,
             50 AS Priority ,
             'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
             'Wait Stats Cleared Recently' AS Finding ,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
             'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run DBCC SQLPERF(''SYS.DM_OS_WAIT_STATS'',CLEAR) ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
       '. Why are you clearing wait stats? What are you hiding?'
       AS Details
     FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
     WHERE d.dbcc_event_full_upper = N'DBCC SQLPERF(''SYS.DM_OS_WAIT_STATS'',CLEAR)'
     GROUP BY COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name)
     HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Performance. More info: https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc

Name: Auto-Shrink Ran Recently
Description: The database auto-shrink operation has run recently, as detected in the default trace. Auto-shrink operations cause severe index fragmentation and I/O overhead. Auto-shrink should be disabled on all production databases.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
SELECT 206 AS CheckID ,
                10 AS Priority ,
                'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
                'Auto-Shrink Ran Recently' AS Finding ,
                '' AS URL ,
          N'The database ' + QUOTENAME(t.DatabaseName) + N' has had '
           + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(10), COUNT(*))
            + N' auto shrink events between '
             + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(t.StartTime)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MAX(t.StartTime))
              + ' that lasted on average '
               + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(10), AVG(DATEDIFF(SECOND, t.StartTime, t.EndTime)))
                + ' seconds.' AS Details
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
      WHERE t.EventClass IN (94, 95)
      GROUP BY t.DatabaseName
      HAVING AVG(DATEDIFF(SECOND, t.StartTime, t.EndTime)) > 5

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Performance. More info: https://www.brentozar.com/blitz/

Name: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently
Description: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS has been run recently, flushing the buffer pool of all clean (unmodified) data pages. This causes subsequent queries to read all data from disk rather than the buffer cache, causing a temporary but significant performance impact.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
SELECT 207 AS CheckID ,
             10 AS Priority ,
             'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
             'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently' AS Finding ,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
             'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
       '. If this is a production box, know that you''re clearing all data out of memory when this happens. What kind of monster would do that?'
       AS Details
     FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
     WHERE d.dbcc_event_full_upper = N'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS'
     GROUP BY COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name)
     HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Performance. More info: https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc

Name: DBCC SHRINK% Ran Recently
Description: A DBCC SHRINKFILE or DBCC SHRINKDATABASE operation has been executed recently. Database shrink operations cause significant index fragmentation, requiring subsequent index rebuild operations to restore performance.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
      SELECT 210 AS CheckID ,
              10 AS Priority ,
              'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
              'DBCC SHRINK% Ran Recently' AS Finding ,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
              'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run file shrinks ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
        '. So, uh, are they trying to cause bad performance on purpose?'
        AS Details
      FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
      WHERE d.dbcc_event_trunc_upper LIKE N'DBCC SHRINK%'
      GROUP BY COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name)
      HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Performance. More info: https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc

Name: High Index Fragmentation
Description: Checks if any indexes have fragmentation > 30%
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 'LIMITED') ips
  WHERE ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30
  AND ips.page_count > 1000
  AND ips.index_id > 0
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Consider rebuilding or reorganizing fragmented indexes

Name: Missing Index Recommendations
Description: Checks if there are missing index recommendations with high impact
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details mid
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups mig ON mid.index_handle = mig.index_handle
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats migs ON mig.index_group_handle = migs.group_handle
  WHERE migs.avg_user_impact > 50
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Review missing index recommendations and create appropriate indexes

Name: There Is An Error With The Default Trace
Description: The SQL Server default trace is experiencing an error and may not be capturing events correctly. The default trace provides important diagnostic information about server events; errors in it represent gaps in the diagnostic record.
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Reliability. More info: https://www.brentozar.com/go/defaulttrace

Name: DBCC WRITEPAGE Used Recently
Description: DBCC WRITEPAGE has been used recently. This is an undocumented DBCC command that directly writes to database pages, bypassing normal SQL Server data integrity mechanisms. Its use is extremely dangerous and can cause data corruption.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
      SELECT 209 AS CheckID ,
              10 AS Priority ,
              'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup ,
              'DBCC WRITEPAGE Used Recently' AS Finding ,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
              'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run DBCC WRITEPAGE ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
        '. So, uh, are they trying to fix corruption, or cause corruption?'
        AS Details
      FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
      WHERE d.dbcc_event_trunc_upper = N'DBCC WRITEPAGE'
      GROUP BY COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name)
      HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Review finding: Reliability. More info: https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc

Name: SA Account Enabled
Description: Checks if the SA account is enabled
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name = 'sa' AND is_disabled = 0)
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Disable the SA account and use named admin accounts instead

Name: Weak Password Policies
Description: Checks if password policy enforcement is disabled for SQL logins
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.sql_logins 
  WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 
  AND name NOT IN ('sa', '##MS_PolicyEventProcessingLogin##')
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Enable password policy enforcement for all SQL logins

Name: Non-Default server configurations - access check cache bucket count
Description: Non-Default server configurations - access check cache bucket count
SqlQuery:
WITH ConfigurationDefaults ([name] ,
  DefaultValue ,
  CheckID ) AS( select * 
 from (
 VALUES
  ( 'access check cache bucket count', 0, 1001 )
 
  ) T([name] ,
  DefaultValue ,
  CheckID )
 )
 SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (


  SELECT cd.CheckID ,
   200 AS Priority ,
   'Non-Default Server Config' AS FindingsGroup ,
   cr.name AS Finding ,
   'https://www.brentozar.com/go/conf' AS URL ,
   ( 'This sp_configure option has been changed. Its default value is '
   + COALESCE(CAST(cd.[DefaultValue] AS VARCHAR(100)),
    '(unknown)')
   + ' and it has been set to '
   + CAST(cr.value_in_use AS VARCHAR(100))
   + '.' ) AS Details
  FROM sys.configurations cr
   INNER JOIN ConfigurationDefaults cd ON cd.name = cr.name
   LEFT OUTER JOIN ConfigurationDefaults cdUsed ON cdUsed.name = cr.name
     AND cdUsed.DefaultValue = cr.value_in_use
  WHERE cdUsed.name IS NULL
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Enter recommended action

      'https://www.brentozar.com/go/conf' AS URL,
      ('This sp_configure option has been changed. Its default value is ' 
       + COALESCE(CAST(cd.[DefaultValue] AS VARCHAR(100)), '(unknown)')
       + ' and it has been set to ' + CAST(cr.value_in_use AS VARCHAR(100)) + '.') AS Details
    FROM sys.configurations cr
    INNER JOIN ConfigurationDefaults cd ON cd.name = cr.name
    LEFT OUTER JOIN ConfigurationDefaults cdUsed ON cdUsed.name = cr.name AND cdUsed.DefaultValue = cr.value_in_use
    WHERE cdUsed.name IS NULL
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Review the 'xp_cmdshell' setting. Default is 0. More info: https://www.brentozar.com/go/conf
DetailedRemediation: The sp_configure option 'xp_cmdshell' has been changed from its default value of 0. Review if this change is intentional and appropriate for your environment.
AdditionalNotes: sp_Blitz CheckID 1065 - Non-Default Server Configuration

SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks wt WHERE wt.blocking_session_id > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
Name: Blocking Detected
RecommendedAction: Review blocking chains below for details
AdditionalNotes: Simple blocking detection for Live Monitor
DetailedRemediation: Active blocking detected. Check Blocking Chains section for details.
Description: Active blocking sessions detected

Name: Auto Shrink Enabled
Description: Checks if any database has AUTO_SHRINK enabled (causes fragmentation)
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_auto_shrink_on = 1 AND database_id > 4)
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Disable AUTO_SHRINK on all user databases

Name: Percentage Growth Settings
Description: Checks if any database files use percentage-based growth (not recommended)
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.master_files 
  WHERE is_percent_growth = 1 
  AND database_id > 4
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Change database file growth from percentage to fixed MB increments

Name: TempDB File Count
Description: Checks if TempDB has fewer files than CPU cores (up to 8)
RecommendedAction: Add more TempDB data files to match CPU cores (up to 8 files)

Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description: Checks for suspect pages indicating corruption
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3))
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Run DBCC CHECKDB immediately and restore from backups if needed
